description: Artisan Roaster Scope feature parity for rustRoast. Implements the highest-impact gaps identified from a comprehensive comparison with Artisan (artisan-scope.org): profile designer RoR simulation, fan speed curve editing, profile transposer, configurable RoR smoothing, auto-calculated phase statistics, roast comparison overlay, cupping notes, data export, and simple alarm/notification system. Uses SvelteKit 2 with Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived, $effect), Tailwind CSS 4 with OKLch dark theme tokens, Apache ECharts (tree-shaken via echarts/core), lucide-svelte icons. Backend is Rust/Axum with SQLite via sqlx. Existing infrastructure: API client at $lib/api/client.ts, telemetry WebSocket store at $lib/stores/telemetry.ts, profile store at $lib/stores/profile.svelte.ts, Chart.svelte wrapper, TelemetryChart.svelte, ProfileDesigner.svelte, KeyTemperatures.svelte, PhaseTimingTable.svelte. CRITICAL: All files MUST be .svelte or .ts — no .tsx, no React. Svelte 5 rune stores use .svelte.ts extension. Follow existing patterns from dashboard components. RESEARCH REQUIREMENT: Before implementing each story, use the Context7 MCP tools (resolve-library-id then query-docs) to look up current best practices for the libraries you are about to use. This ensures modern, idiomatic code instead of stale patterns. Specifically: (1) Before any Svelte component work, query Context7 for 'svelte' to verify Svelte 5 runes patterns ($state, $derived, $effect, $props, snippet), component lifecycle, and reactive declarations. (2) Before any ECharts work (charts, radar, series config, markLine, graphic overlays), query Context7 for 'echarts' with the specific chart feature being implemented. (3) Before any Axum route, extractor, or middleware work, query Context7 for 'axum' with the specific pattern (e.g., streaming responses for CSV export, JSON extractors, error handling). (4) Before any SQLx query or transaction, query Context7 for 'sqlx' with the specific operation (e.g., transactions, ALTER TABLE, query_as macros). (5) Before any Tailwind CSS 4 styling questions, query Context7 for 'tailwindcss' to verify utility class names and v4 syntax. Do NOT skip this research step — outdated API usage causes subtle bugs. Limit to 2-3 Context7 lookups per story to stay focused.

userStories.title: Enhanced session completion statistics
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want the system to automatically calculate detailed statistics when a session completes — including development time ratio, weight loss percentage, max RoR, and per-phase average RoR — so I have a complete analytical summary without manual entry.
acceptanceCriteria: complete_session() in services.rs auto-calculates development_time_ratio from roast_events: DTR = (total_time - first_crack_start_elapsed) / total_time if first_crack_start event exists

New columns added to roast_sessions: weight_loss_pct (REAL), max_ror (REAL), avg_ror_drying (REAL), avg_ror_maillard (REAL), avg_ror_development (REAL), drying_end_time (INTEGER), drying_end_temp (REAL)
complete_session() computes weight_loss_pct = (green_weight - roasted_weight) / green_weight * 100 when both weights are present
complete_session() computes max_ror from MAX(rate_of_rise) in session_telemetry for the session

complete_session() computes per-phase avg RoR by querying session_telemetry within each phase time range (phases defined by roast_events: start→drying_end, drying_end→first_crack_start, first_crack_start→drop)
complete_session() copies drying_end elapsed_seconds and temperature from roast_events into session fields for quick access
RoastSession model in models.rs updated with new Option<f32>/Option<i32> fields
GET /api/sessions/:id returns all new fields in the JSON response
cargo test passes with test for complete_session computing DTR and weight loss
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: The complete_session method is in crates/server/src/services.rs. It already calculates max_temp and total_time_seconds from telemetry. Extend the same transaction to query roast_events for the session to get phase boundaries, then query session_telemetry for avg(rate_of_rise) within each time range. Add columns via ALTER TABLE in init_db (use IF NOT EXISTS pattern — SQLite doesn't support it for ALTER TABLE, so wrap each ALTER in a separate query that silently fails if column exists, e.g. check sqlite_master). The UpdateSessionRequest already has first_crack_time and development_time_ratio as optional fields — auto-calculation should overwrite these if events exist. Frontend types in session.ts and client.ts will need the new fields added.

userStories.title: Add AUC computation to session telemetry
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want Area Under the Curve (AUC) computed for my roast sessions so I can compare total thermal energy input across roasts with different profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: New column auc_value (REAL) added to roast_sessions table
AUC calculated using trapezoidal rule on bean_temp from session_telemetry: sum of ((t[i+1] - t[i]) * (BT[i] + BT[i+1]) / 2) for all consecutive telemetry points, with base temperature subtracted
AUC base temperature defaults to 0°C (configurable via settings key 'auc_base_temp', seeded to '0')
AUC start point defaults to CHARGE (elapsed_seconds >= 0), configurable via settings key 'auc_start_event' (values: 'charge', 'drying_end', 'first_crack_start')
AUC result stored in °C·min units (divide seconds-based integral by 60)
complete_session() computes and stores auc_value in the same transaction as other stats
GET /api/sessions/:id returns auc_value in the JSON response
Default settings seeded in init_db: auc_base_temp='0', auc_start_event='charge'
cargo test passes with test for AUC computation (simple 3-point linear curve with known area)
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: The trapezoidal rule is straightforward: query session_telemetry ORDER BY elapsed_seconds, iterate pairwise, accumulate. Base temp subtraction: for each sample, use max(0, bean_temp - base_temp) to avoid negative contributions. The AUC start event means we skip telemetry before the relevant event's elapsed_seconds. Settings are read via the existing settings table (SELECT value FROM settings WHERE key = ?). Add auc_base_temp and auc_start_event defaults in init_db alongside profile_lookahead_seconds. Result unit is °C·min — Artisan uses this unit for comparability. Frontend types need auc_value added to RoastSession.

userStories.title: Add RoR smoothing settings to backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need backend settings for RoR smoothing configuration so the frontend can persist user preferences for delta span and smoothing algorithm.
acceptanceCriteria: Default settings seeded in init_db: ror_window_seconds='30', ror_smoothing_algorithm='moving_average'
Supported ror_smoothing_algorithm values: 'moving_average', 'weighted_moving_average', 'savitzky_golay'
Settings are stored/retrieved via existing GET/PUT /api/settings endpoints (no new endpoints needed)
cargo test passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: This is a minimal backend story — just seed the default settings in init_db using INSERT OR IGNORE. The actual smoothing computation happens in the frontend telemetry store (AP-007). The backend only persists the preference. Add the two INSERT OR IGNORE statements alongside the existing profile_lookahead_seconds seed.

userStories.title: Profile designer RoR simulation curve
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see the simulated Rate of Rise curve computed from my designed temperature profile so I can evaluate if the RoR shape is viable before running the roast. This is the single highest-value profile design feature — roasters make all decisions based on RoR shape, not temperature.
acceptanceCriteria: ProfileDesigner.svelte computes a simulated RoR curve from the designed profile points
RoR is calculated as the numerical first derivative: for each pair of consecutive sorted points, RoR = (temp[i+1] - temp[i]) / (time[i+1] - time[i]) * 60 (°C/min)
RoR curve interpolated at 5-second intervals across the profile duration for smooth display
RoR displayed as a second Y-axis (right side) with green dashed line (#34d399, lineStyle: { type: 'dashed' })
RoR Y-axis range auto-adjusts with sensible bounds (typically 0-30 °C/min for coffee roasting)
RoR curve updates in real-time as user adds, moves, or deletes profile points
Legend shows 'Simulated RoR' entry alongside the temperature curve
Phase boundary markers shown as vertical dashed lines at configured phase temperatures (default: DRY at 150°C, FC at target_first_crack or 200°C) with labels
DTR preview calculated from FC marker to final point and displayed below the chart: 'Est. DTR: XX%'
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The RoR simulation is a pure computation from the profile points — no backend needed. Algorithm: (1) sort points by time_seconds, (2) for each 5-second interval from 0 to max_time, interpolate BT using linear interpolation between surrounding points (reuse interpolateTarget from profile.svelte.ts), (3) compute central difference derivative: RoR[t] = (BT[t+5] - BT[t-5]) / 10 * 60, or forward/backward difference at boundaries. This gives a smooth RoR curve. Add as a second series in the ECharts config. Use yAxisIndex: 1 for the RoR axis (add a second yAxis to the chart). Phase markers: find the time where interpolated temp crosses 150°C (dry) and target_first_crack (FC). Use ECharts markLine on the temperature series for vertical lines. DTR = (total_time - fc_time) / total_time * 100. Display below chart in a stats bar. The ProfileDesigner currently uses a single yAxis — add a second one for RoR. Make the RoR series non-interactive (silent: true) so it doesn't interfere with point editing.

userStories.title: Profile designer fan speed curve editing
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to design and visualize the fan speed curve alongside the temperature curve so I can plan airflow management as part of my roast profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Fan speed series displayed as a stepped line on the profile designer chart (step: 'end' type in ECharts)
Fan speed uses a third Y-axis or shares the RoR right axis with range 0-100%
Fan speed color: purple (#a78bfa) with area fill at 15% opacity (matching TelemetryChart fan style)
Fan speed is editable per profile point in the table view (fan_speed column, 0-100 range)
When a point is selected on the chart, the point editor shows a fan_speed input field alongside time and temp
Fan speed defaults to null for new points (not rendered until explicitly set)
Fan speed curve visible in legend as 'Fan Speed'
Fan speed values saved correctly via profiles.create() and profiles.update() API calls
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The profile_points table already has a fan_speed column (INTEGER, 0-100). The ProfileDesigner already has a fan_speed input in the table view. This story adds the visual chart representation. Use ECharts step line series: { name: 'Fan Speed', type: 'line', step: 'end', data: sortedPoints.filter(p => p.fan_speed !== null).map(p => [p.time_seconds, p.fan_speed]), yAxisIndex: 1, areaStyle: { opacity: 0.15 }, itemStyle: { color: '#a78bfa' }, silent: true }. The fan speed shares the right Y-axis (0-100 range works for both RoR and fan percentage). Ensure fan_speed null points are excluded from the series data to avoid gaps.

userStories.title: Profile transposer dialog
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to scale and shift a profile's time and temperature curves so I can adapt profiles for different batch sizes, bean densities, or target roast levels without manually editing every point.
acceptanceCriteria: New ProfileTransposer.svelte component at $lib/components/ProfileTransposer.svelte
Accessible from the profile edit page via a 'Transpose' button
Time scaling: input field for scale factor (e.g., 0.8 = 20% faster, 1.2 = 20% slower). All point time_seconds multiplied by factor and rounded to nearest integer.
Temperature shift: input field for offset in °C (e.g., +5 shifts entire curve up 5°C). All target_temp values adjusted by offset.
Temperature scale: input field for scale factor around the mean (e.g., 1.1 = 10% more spread). Each temp adjusted: new_temp = mean + (temp - mean) * factor.
Preview chart shows the original curve (dashed gray) alongside the transposed curve (solid amber) for visual comparison
Apply button updates the profile points in the designer (does not save to DB — user must click Save separately)
Cancel button closes the dialog without changes
Simulated RoR updates for the transposed preview curve
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Implement as a modal/dialog component that receives the current points array and returns the transformed points array. Artisan supports discrete/linear/quadratic mapping between landmark events — start with simpler linear scale+shift which covers 90% of use cases. The preview chart is a standalone ECharts instance inside the dialog (not the main designer chart). Use the Chart.svelte wrapper. Original points rendered as dashed gray line, transposed as solid amber. On Apply, emit the transformed points back to the parent ProfileDesigner which replaces its points state. The transposer opens in a modal (use a simple backdrop div with z-50, same pattern as other dialogs in the app). Time scaling should clamp minimum to 1 second between points.

userStories.title: Configurable RoR smoothing in frontend
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to configure the RoR smoothing window and algorithm so I can tune the delta curve to be useful for my specific roaster's noise profile.
acceptanceCriteria: RoR smoothing settings added to the settings page (/settings): window size (5-120 seconds, step 5) and algorithm selector (Moving Average, Weighted Moving Average, Savitzky-Golay)
Settings persisted via existing PUT /api/settings/{key} for ror_window_seconds and ror_smoothing_algorithm
Telemetry store's rateOfRise derived computation updated to use the configured window size instead of hardcoded 30 seconds
Moving Average: simple mean of bean_temp values in window (current behavior)
Weighted Moving Average: linearly weighted — more recent samples weighted higher. Weight[i] = i / sum(1..n)

Savitzky-Golay: 2nd-order polynomial fit over the window, derivative taken analytically. Use least-squares polyfit on the window points, then evaluate the polynomial's first derivative at the center point.
Algorithm selection loaded from settings on telemetry store initialization
RoR value still displayed in CurrentReadings and TelemetryChart with same formatting
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: The current RoR calculation in telemetry.ts uses a 30-second window with simple (lastTemp - firstTemp) / dt * 60. Replace with a function that accepts the algorithm name and window size. For Savitzky-Golay, implement a minimal version: given N points (time, temp), fit a polynomial p(t) = a + bt + ct², then RoR = b + 2ct evaluated at the center time, scaled to °C/min. This can be done with a 3x3 matrix solve or precomputed convolution coefficients for uniform spacing (since telemetry is ~1Hz). Load settings once on mount via settings.get() and store in module-level $state variables. The settings page input follows the same pattern as the lookahead setting (PF-010). Keep the settings page clean — group under a 'Rate of Rise' subsection.

userStories.title: Phase statistics panel for live and historical roasts
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see detailed phase statistics — drying, Maillard, and development times with percentages and average RoR per phase — both live during a roast and on the session detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: PhaseTimingTable.svelte enhanced to show: duration (MM:SS), percentage of total time, average RoR (°C/min), and temperature rise (°C) for each phase
Phase statistics computed from session telemetry data: avg RoR = mean(rate_of_rise) for telemetry points within phase time range, temp rise = phase_end_temp - phase_start_temp
For completed sessions on the detail page: statistics read from the pre-computed fields on roast_sessions (avg_ror_drying, avg_ror_maillard, avg_ror_development from AP-001)
For live roasts on the dashboard: statistics computed in real-time from telemetryHistory store data using the same algorithm
New PhaseStatsPanel.svelte component that combines PhaseTimingTable data with RoR and temp rise in a clean table format
Panel shows Artisan-style phase colors: green (drying), yellow (Maillard), brown (development)
Development Time Ratio (DTR) displayed prominently below the phase table: 'DTR: XX.X%'
Panel used on both /sessions/[id] detail page and the live dashboard
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Enhance the existing PhaseTimingTable or create a new PhaseStatsPanel that replaces it. The component needs two modes: (1) historical mode — receives pre-computed stats from the session object (AP-001 fields), (2) live mode — receives telemetryHistory array and roast events, computes stats in $derived. For live mode, filter telemetryHistory by phase boundaries from events and compute mean(bean_temp delta). Phase colors from Artisan: Drying = #22c55e (green-500), Maillard = #eab308 (yellow-500), Development = #92400e (amber-900/brown). The session detail page at /sessions/[id]/+page.svelte already uses PhaseTimingTable — replace or augment it. DTR = development_time / total_time * 100.

userStories.title: Weight loss and AUC display on session detail
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see weight loss percentage and AUC value on the session detail page so I can evaluate roast quality metrics at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Weight loss displayed on session detail page when both green_weight and roasted_weight are present
Weight loss format: 'Weight Loss: XX.X% (YYYg → ZZZg)' showing input/output weights
AUC displayed on session detail page when auc_value is present: 'AUC: XXX °C·min'
Max RoR displayed: 'Max RoR: XX.X °C/min'
Metrics displayed in a summary card/grid alongside existing KeyTemperatures component

Frontend types updated: SessionWithTelemetry and RoastSession interfaces include weight_loss_pct, auc_value, max_ror, avg_ror_drying, avg_ror_maillard, avg_ror_development, drying_end_time, drying_end_temp
Missing metrics gracefully hidden (not shown as 'N/A' — just omitted)
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: This is purely a frontend display story. The backend already computes and returns these values (AP-001 and AP-002). Add a new RoastMetrics.svelte component or extend KeyTemperatures.svelte. The session detail page at /sessions/[id]/+page.svelte fetches SessionWithTelemetry which includes the session object with all fields. Use a grid of metric cards similar to KeyTemperatures (bg-card rounded-lg p-3 pattern). Update the TypeScript interfaces in both $lib/types/session.ts and $lib/api/client.ts to include the new optional fields.

userStories.title: Roast comparison overlay page
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to overlay 2-3 roast sessions on a single chart aligned by time or a roast event so I can compare how different roasts performed and identify patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: New route /sessions/compare with page component
Session selector allows picking 2-3 sessions from a dropdown or searchable list
Each selected session's telemetry loaded via sessions.get(id) and rendered as a separate series on a shared ECharts chart
Alignment options: 'By Time' (default, aligned from session start) or 'By Event' (aligned to a selected event type: drying_end, first_crack_start, drop)
Event alignment shifts each session's time axis so the selected event occurs at t=0
Each session rendered in a distinct color (auto-assigned from a palette: amber, blue, green, red, purple)
Both BT and RoR curves shown per session (toggleable via legend checkboxes)
Metadata summary table below chart showing: session name, date, bean, weight loss, DTR, AUC, max temp for each session
Link to comparison page from session detail page ('Compare with...' button) and sessions list page (checkbox selection + 'Compare' button)
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Create a new route at apps/dashboard/src/routes/sessions/compare/+page.svelte. Pass session IDs via URL query params: /sessions/compare?ids=id1,id2,id3. Load telemetry for each session via sessions.get(). For the chart, create multiple series pairs (BT + RoR) per session, each with a unique color from a predefined palette. For event alignment, find the elapsed_seconds of the chosen event in each session's events array, then subtract that offset from all telemetry elapsed_seconds. Use an ECharts instance with multi-series configuration. The metadata table uses a simple HTML table with Tailwind styling. The color palette should have 5 distinct colors to support up to 5 sessions. The session list page can add checkboxes + a floating 'Compare (N)' button that navigates to /sessions/compare?ids=...

userStories.title: Auto-event detection (AutoDRY, AutoFC)
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want the system to automatically suggest DRY END and First Crack Start events based on characteristic RoR patterns so I don't have to manually press buttons at the exact moment.
acceptanceCriteria: AutoDRY detection: when bean_temp crosses a configurable threshold (default 150°C, stored in settings as 'auto_dry_temp'), a notification suggests marking DRY END
AutoFC detection: when RoR drops significantly after sustained rise (detect when RoR falls below 80% of its 30-second moving average for 3+ consecutive seconds), suggest First Crack Start
Suggestions appear as non-blocking toast notifications with 'Mark Event' and 'Dismiss' buttons
Clicking 'Mark Event' creates the roast event via events.create() with the detected elapsed_seconds and temperature
Each auto-detection fires at most once per session (tracked in session-level state)
Auto-detection only active during active sessions (status === 'active')
Auto-detection can be disabled via settings toggle: 'auto_event_detection' (default 'true')
Settings seeded in init_db: auto_dry_temp='150', auto_event_detection='true'
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Implement as a $effect in a new AutoEventDetector.svelte component (or within the dashboard page). Subscribe to the telemetry store and check conditions on each update. AutoDRY is simple: watch bean_temp cross the threshold. AutoFC is more nuanced — track a rolling 30s RoR average and detect when instantaneous RoR drops below 80% of it for 3 consecutive readings. Use a module-level $state Set to track which events have already been suggested this session (reset on session change). The toast notification can reuse the existing notifications store if one exists, or create a simple floating notification component. Settings loaded on mount via settings.get(). The backend needs the two new default settings seeded.

userStories.title: Add cupping notes model and CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend model and API for storing cupping scores and tasting notes associated with roast sessions so the frontend can present a cupping editor.
acceptanceCriteria: New cupping_scores table: id (TEXT PK), session_id (TEXT FK CASCADE), scoring_framework (TEXT DEFAULT 'sca'), overall_score (REAL), notes (TEXT), created_at, updated_at
New cupping_attributes table: id (TEXT PK), cupping_id (TEXT FK CASCADE), attribute_name (TEXT NOT NULL), score (REAL NOT NULL), created_at
Default SCA cupping attributes supported: fragrance_aroma, flavor, aftertaste, acidity, body, balance, uniformity, clean_cup, sweetness, overall
POST /api/sessions/:session_id/cupping — create cupping score with attributes (upsert: replaces if exists for session)
GET /api/sessions/:session_id/cupping — returns cupping score with all attributes for a session (404 if none)
DELETE /api/sessions/:session_id/cupping — removes cupping score and attributes for a session
CuppingScore and CuppingAttribute models added to models.rs
CreateCuppingRequest DTO: scoring_framework (optional, defaults 'sca'), notes (optional), attributes: Vec<{ name: String, score: f32 }>
GET /api/sessions/:session_id returns cupping data nested in the response when present
cargo test passes with test for cupping CRUD round-trip
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Create tables in init_db. SCA scoring uses 0-10 scale with 0.25 increments (10 attributes, max total 100). The POST endpoint should be idempotent for a session — delete existing cupping data and recreate. Service methods: create_cupping(session_id, req), get_cupping(session_id), delete_cupping(session_id). The create method runs in a transaction: DELETE FROM cupping_scores WHERE session_id=?, DELETE FROM cupping_attributes WHERE cupping_id IN (...), then INSERT new data. Return the cupping data as part of get_session_with_telemetry by doing a LEFT JOIN or separate query. Frontend types will be added in the frontend story.

userStories.title: Cupping notes editor with radar chart
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to score my roast using SCA cupping parameters and see the results as a radar/spider chart so I can correlate flavor with roast data.
acceptanceCriteria: New CuppingEditor.svelte component at $lib/components/CuppingEditor.svelte
Radar chart (ECharts radar type) displaying 10 SCA cupping attributes on axes
Each attribute scored 0-10 with 0.25 step via slider or number input
Overall score auto-calculated: sum of all 10 attributes
Free-text notes textarea for cupping observations
Save button calls POST /api/sessions/:session_id/cupping
Load existing cupping data on component mount via GET /api/sessions/:session_id/cupping
Delete button to remove cupping data with confirmation
Radar chart updates in real-time as scores are adjusted
Component used on session detail page (/sessions/[id]) in a collapsible section
Frontend API client methods: cupping.get(sessionId), cupping.create(sessionId, req), cupping.delete(sessionId)
TypeScript interfaces for CuppingScore, CuppingAttribute, CreateCuppingRequest added to types
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: ECharts radar chart config: { radar: { indicator: attributes.map(a => ({ name: a.label, max: 10 })), shape: 'polygon' }, series: [{ type: 'radar', data: [{ value: scores }] }] }. Use the Chart.svelte wrapper. SCA attributes with display labels: Fragrance/Aroma, Flavor, Aftertaste, Acidity, Body, Balance, Uniformity, Clean Cup, Sweetness, Overall. Place on the session detail page below the telemetry chart in a collapsible section (use a <details> element or toggle button). The collapsible starts closed. Frontend API client adds a 'cupping' namespace. Load via $effect on mount.

userStories.title: Add session data export endpoints (CSV, Artisan JSON)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need backend endpoints to export session telemetry and metadata in CSV and Artisan-compatible JSON (.alog) formats for interoperability.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/sessions/:id/export/csv — returns CSV with Content-Disposition: attachment header
CSV format: columns elapsed_seconds, bean_temp, env_temp, rate_of_rise, heater_pwm, fan_pwm, setpoint, with header row
CSV includes session metadata as comment rows at the top: # Session: name, # Date: start_time, # Bean: origin/variety, # Profile: profile_name
GET /api/sessions/:id/export/artisan — returns Artisan-compatible JSON (.alog format)
Artisan JSON includes: timex array (seconds), temp1 array (ET), temp2 array (BT), specialevents array (from roast_events), roastdate, title, beans, weight (green/roasted)
Artisan JSON timeindex array maps standard events to their index positions in the timex array: [CHARGE, DRY, FCs, FCe, SCs, SCe, DROP, COOL]
Both endpoints require auth (bearer token)
cargo test passes with test for CSV and Artisan JSON export format correctness
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: For CSV: use a simple string builder — no external crate needed. Set Content-Type: text/csv and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="{session_name}_{date}.csv". For Artisan JSON: the .alog format uses Python-style JSON (single quotes in older versions, but modern Artisan accepts standard JSON). Key fields: 'timex' = [elapsed_seconds array], 'temp1' = [env_temp array], 'temp2' = [bean_temp array], 'timeindex' = [8 integers mapping event positions], 'roastdate' = ISO date string, 'title' = session name, 'beans' = origin info, 'weight' = [green_weight, roasted_weight, unit_string]. The timeindex maps: index 0=CHARGE, 1=DRY, 2=FCs, 3=FCe, 4=SCs, 5=SCe, 6=DROP, 7=COOL. Find the nearest telemetry index for each event's elapsed_seconds. Use -1 for missing events. This completes the import/export round-trip (rustRoast already imports .alog).

userStories.title: Data export UI
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want download buttons on the session detail page to export my roast data as CSV or Artisan JSON (.alog) so I can share, archive, or analyze in other tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Export dropdown button on session detail page with options: 'Download CSV', 'Download Artisan (.alog)'
CSV download triggers GET /api/sessions/:id/export/csv and saves as file via browser download
Artisan download triggers GET /api/sessions/:id/export/artisan and saves as .alog file
File names follow pattern: {session_name}_{YYYY-MM-DD}.csv or .alog
Loading state shown during export (spinner on button)
Error notification on export failure
Export button only shown for completed sessions (status === 'completed')
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: For browser file downloads, use fetch() to get the response, create a Blob, generate a URL via URL.createObjectURL(), create an anchor element, set href and download attributes, click programmatically, then revoke the URL. The export dropdown can use a simple button group or a native <select> styled with Tailwind. Place alongside the existing 'Delete' and 'Save as Profile' buttons on the session detail page header. Use lucide-svelte icons: Download for the export button. The API endpoints require auth — pass the bearer token in the fetch header (use the existing request() helper from client.ts or add an exportBlob helper).

userStories.title: Simple alarm and notification system
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to set temperature and time-based alarms that trigger visual and audio notifications during a roast so I'm alerted at critical moments without watching the screen constantly.
acceptanceCriteria: New alarms settings section on the settings page with a table of configurable alarms

Each alarm has: name (string), condition_type ('temp_above', 'temp_below', 'time_after_event', 'ror_below'), threshold (number), reference_event (optional, for time-based: 'charge', 'drying_end', 'first_crack_start'), enabled (boolean)
Default alarms seeded: 'High Temp Warning' (temp_above 230°C), 'FC Approaching' (temp_above 195°C), 'Low RoR Warning' (ror_below 5.0 after first_crack_start)
Alarms stored in settings as JSON string under key 'roast_alarms'
AlarmMonitor.svelte component evaluates alarms against live telemetry each tick
Triggered alarms show a toast notification with the alarm name and current value
Optional browser audio alert (short beep) using Web Audio API — toggleable via setting 'alarm_sound_enabled' (default 'true')
Each alarm fires at most once per session
Alarms only evaluated during active sessions
Alarm management UI: add, edit, delete, enable/disable alarms in the settings table
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Store alarms as a JSON array in the settings table under key 'roast_alarms'. This avoids a new DB table and keeps the schema simple. The AlarmMonitor component loads the alarm configuration from settings on mount, then uses $effect to evaluate conditions on each telemetry update. For temp_above: trigger when beanTemp > threshold. For ror_below: trigger when rateOfRise < threshold AND the reference event has been marked. For time_after_event: trigger when elapsed_seconds > event_elapsed + threshold. Use a Set<string> to track fired alarm names per session. The toast notification system can be a simple fixed-position div at the top of the screen with auto-dismiss (5 seconds). For audio: new AudioContext(), create a short oscillator beep (440Hz, 200ms). The settings page alarm table uses inline editing with Tailwind-styled inputs.
